tasks.task_name: Find warm backpacking destination (1-2 night, mid-March)
tasks.first_domino: Google 'warm backpacking March weekend US' and open map
tasks.hook_line: Can you find a warm backpacking spot that's actually accessible in 5 days with no gear?
tasks.reward: Locks in the weekend plan and unblocks flight booking
nuggets: Big Bend TX hits 75°F mid-March
Red River Gorge KY 60°F, easier permits
Joshua Tree CA warm but gear rental closer
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find a warm backpacking destination for a 1-2 night trip the weekend of March 14-15, 2026. I'm in San Francisco. Looking for somewhere that's warm (60°F+), beginner-friendly trails, doesn't require advance permits, and is reachable by Friday evening. After the trip I need to fly to Chicago by Sunday evening. I don't own backpacking gear yet (figuring out REI rentals separately). Give me 3-4 options with temp forecasts, trail suggestions, flight logistics from SF, and how to get from trailhead to Chicago.

tasks.task_name: Figure out REI gear rentals (backpack, tent, sleeping bag)
tasks.first_domino: Open REI.com and search 'backpacking gear rental'
tasks.hook_line: REI rents complete backpacking kits — do they have everything you need in one package?
tasks.reward: Answers the gear question so you can focus on destination and flights
nuggets: REI rents backpack+tent+sleeping bag kits
Pick up locally, return at any store
Weekend rental ~$70-90 total
tasks.system_prompt: Help me understand REI's backpacking gear rental program. I need a backpack, tent, and sleeping bag for a 1-2 night trip March 14-15. I'm in San Francisco. What do they offer in complete kits? What are the costs? How does pickup and return work? Can I pick up Thursday evening and return Monday? What's NOT included that I'd need to buy or bring separately? Give me a clear breakdown of what this would cost and what the process looks like.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights with SF and destination airport
tasks.hook_line: 4 days out — flight prices are climbing every hour
tasks.reward: Commits to the destination and makes the trip real
nuggets: Friday evening departure needed
Book both legs together for better price
Southwest allows free cancellation 10min before
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book flights for a backpacking trip March 14-15. Departing SF Friday evening (after work, ideally 6pm+), returning Sunday to connect to Chicago. Once I tell you the destination, find me flight options on Google Flights, optimize for price and timing, and walk me through booking. I prefer Southwest for flexibility but open to others if significantly cheaper.

tasks.task_name: Book flight from backpacking to Chicago
tasks.first_domino: Open Google Flights with backpacking airport to Chicago
tasks.hook_line: Sunday evening flight to Chicago — cutting it close for birthday weekend
tasks.reward: Completes the full travel loop and you can stop thinking about logistics
nuggets: ORD and MDW both work for Chicago
Need to land by evening for birthday prep
Book same airline as outbound if possible
tasks.system_prompt: I need to book a flight from my backpacking destination to Chicago on Sunday March 15, arriving by evening. Once I tell you the departure airport, find me options on Google Flights. Mariano's 30th birthday party is that weekend so I need to be there Sunday evening. Walk me through the best options for price and timing.

tasks.first_domino: Text Mariano's girlfriend to ask what he wants
tasks.hook_line: First 30th birthday in the friend group — what do you even get?
tasks.reward: Shows up prepared and avoids last-minute Chicago airport gift panic
nuggets: Ask his partner for ideas first
Chicago delivery available if buying online
Experience gifts work for milestone birthdays
tasks.system_prompt: Help me figure out a 30th birthday gift for my friend Mariano. We're doing a backpacking trip together then celebrating in Chicago. I don't know if he even wants gifts or if the trip is enough. Give me strategies for: 1) How to ask without being awkward, 2) Gift ideas in the $50-150 range if he does want something, 3) Whether experience gifts or physical items make more sense for a milestone birthday.

tasks.task_name: Validate insurance/prescription requirements for buying 4 pairs of glasses
tasks.first_domino: Email insurance agent: subject 'Japan eyewear coverage question'
tasks.hook_line: Can you actually use US insurance to buy 4 pairs of glasses in Japan and save hundreds?
tasks.reward: Validates the whole glasses shopping plan before you're standing in a Tokyo store
nuggets: Vision insurance usually 1 pair/year limit
International receipts need specific format
Prescription needs to be less than 1yr old
tasks.system_prompt: Help me draft an email to my vision insurance agent (VSP or similar). I'm traveling to Japan March 23 - April 7 and want to buy 4 pairs of eyeglasses there because prices are much cheaper. Questions to ask: 1) Can I use my insurance benefit for glasses purchased in Japan? 2) What documentation do I need (receipts, prescriptions)? 3) Is there a limit on how many pairs I can claim in a year? 4) Do I need a new prescription or is my current one valid? Make it brief and professional.

tasks.task_name: Look up eyeglasses stores in Kyoto for early trip shopping
tasks.hook_line: Kyoto has 2000+ temples — but where are the good eyewear shops hidden?
tasks.reward: Gets glasses shopping done early so you're not carrying them through Takayama and Tokyo
nuggets: Zoff and JINS chain stores English-friendly
Glasses ready same-day or next-day
Kyoto downtown has 3+ major optical shops
tasks.system_prompt: Help me find eyeglasses stores in Kyoto, Japan. I'm visiting March 25-27 and want to buy 4 pairs of glasses early in the trip. Need stores that: 1) Speak English or have English-speaking staff, 2) Offer same-day or next-day service, 3) Have good selection and modern frames, 4) Are located in central Kyoto near hotels/tourist areas. Give me 3-5 specific shops with addresses, Google Maps links, hours, and what they're known for.

tasks.first_domino: Open Google Maps and search 'Kyoto temples walking route'
tasks.hook_line: 14 days out and Kyoto is still a blank page — 2.5 days to fill
tasks.reward: Closes out Kyoto planning — only Takayama and Tokyo left
nuggets: Fushimi Inari free, open 24hrs, best at dawn
Arashiyama bamboo grove best before 9am crowds
Kinkaku-ji ¥500 entry, opens 9am, gets packed
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 2.5 days in Kyoto, March 25-27, 2026. Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like temples, walking, outdoor spaces, good food, and avoiding crowds. Already have hotels booked in central Kyoto. I want a day-by-day itinerary with: 1) Mix of major landmarks and less touristy spots, 2) Opening hours and entry fees, 3) Google Maps links, 4) Suggested walking routes between sites, 5) Restaurant recommendations for lunch/dinner. We're okay with early mornings to beat crowds.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Takayama hiking trails March' and open map
tasks.hook_line: Takayama is the mountain town between Kyoto and Tokyo — what's the hidden gem there?
tasks.reward: Completes the middle section of the trip and you know what to expect
nuggets: Old town preserved Edo-period streets
Morning markets famous for local crafts
Hida beef specialty, try local restaurants
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities in Takayama, Japan, for 1-2 days (dates flexible, late March 2026). Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. We like hiking, traditional architecture, local food, and less touristy experiences. What should we do in Takayama? Give me: 1) Top activities and sights, 2) Any good hiking trails nearby (we're moderately fit), 3) Food specialties to try, 4) How much time to spend there, 5) Google Maps links and practical details.

tasks.first_domino: Google 'Tokyo neighborhoods first-time visitor' and open map
tasks.hook_line: Tokyo is massive and overwhelming — where do you even start for a first visit?
tasks.reward: Finishes all activity planning for the entire Japan trip
nuggets: Tsukiji outer market best breakfast spot
Shibuya/Harajuku youth culture and shopping
Asakusa for traditional Tokyo temple vibes
tasks.system_prompt: Help me plan activities for 4-5 days in Tokyo, late March/early April 2026. Traveling with my girlfriend Dana. First time in Tokyo. We like: neighborhoods to explore on foot, good food (especially breakfast and lunch spots), interesting shops (fountain pens, stationery, watches), parks/outdoor spaces, and a mix of modern and traditional. NOT interested in: anime/manga culture, nightlife/clubs, overly touristy traps. Give me a day-by-day framework with neighborhoods to explore, specific spots to hit, food recommendations, and Google Maps links.

tasks.first_domino: Clone the Cerebro repo and open README.md
tasks.hook_line: Developer portal that won't run locally — can you debug the setup before anyone else does?
tasks.reward: Unblocks all future Cerebro work and you can finally explore the codebase
nuggets: Internal tool for engineers, likely Docker setup
Check Slack for setup docs or ask team
Might need VPN or internal credentials
tasks.system_prompt: Help me get the Cerebro developer portal running on my local machine. It's a work project I haven't touched since November 2025. I don't remember the setup steps. Walk me through: 1) What to check in the README or docs, 2) Common issues with running internal tools locally (Docker, env vars, credentials, VPN), 3) Who to ask if I get stuck (team channels, setup docs), 4) How to verify it's actually running correctly. Assume I'm starting fresh and need step-by-step guidance.

recommended_slots.reason: 4 days out, unblocks all other Mariano trip logistics, medium effort fits ramp_up energy
Quick win, low context cost, validates entire glasses shopping strategy before trip
Low effort research, pairs well with destination search, answers gear question
